20 minutes in, I already started hating the movie. By 30 minutes in, I was already debating whether to walk or not. I decided to stay, which was a mistake. A few race jokes here and there is fine. But to ram the whole "black" scenario every 3 seconds gets old real quick. Also, there was absolutely no plot to the movie and the acting was stupid (even the original cast was stupid. Whether to match the stupidity of the young cast or not is questionable). There were no scene(s) that I, or anyone else in the entire theater, found funny.
Hilarious, they did a great job with this. Definitely worth the watch.

It is a fantasy fun movie. The lead character on his quest for the power of a sword goes through various challenges to reach his goal. I liked that story placed him in normal society of today instead of a separate living area. He faces the same challenges of work, bosses etc as everyone but a personal goal to find the sword. The evil character is intense to me. Fun movie
I'm from that last generation who grew up watching He-Man and this movie was just OK. A lot sarcasm but no bad at all. What I really loved was the whole Eternia environment and the characters.
Definitely really fun. Great visuals. A little campy, but on purpose to play into the time period that birthed it. It’s not elevating the action fantasy genre, but it’s definitely a fun ride.
Took me instantly back to my childhood. I really loved this movie. My kids loved this movie too. Very exciting movie. Skeletor was hilarious too. I'll watch it again!
I haven’t seen a movie this bad in a long time.

*SPOILERS* As much as I can't stand Jared Leto, he played Skeletor very well. Teela was absolutely amazing. Man-at-Arms, brilliant. The battle bot was great. Other than that, what I thought was going to be a fun movie fell pretty short. CGI was good but the plot, meh. I wish Orco and Cringer would have been more present in this movie. I don't even remember Fisto and I wish they would have put Man-E-Faces instead.

Masters of the Universe was a fun and entertaining movie that delivered the action, adventure, and fantasy elements I was hoping for. The visuals were strong, the world looked great, and it did a solid job bringing the franchise to life. My biggest criticism is that some of the acting felt a little off at times. There were a few moments where the performances didn’t quite match the intensity of the scenes, which occasionally took me out of the movie. Thankfully, it wasn’t enough to hurt the overall experience. Despite those issues, I had a good time watching it. It’s the kind of movie that’s easy to sit back, relax, and enjoy without overthinking. Not perfect, but definitely a fun ride. Rating: 8/10

Wow absolutely amazing! I loved every character portrayal and their designs, Jared Leto did an amazing job as skeletor he is very comical in the cartoon and has been memed a ton and for them to slip that in was brilliant. The main actor that play He-Man loved him, the love interest and dynamic loved it, and my favorite character shows up at the end with the battle Cat! All in all and impressive Blend of comedy and action while staying true to the franchise, a class act in film.
I love it, a very fun movie. I recommend it especially those who loved He-Man as a kid. 10/10
as never seen the original I enjoyed the premise and has made me wanna watch the old to see how I stands up

If you can imagine watching YouTuber CoryxKenshin play a video game adaptation of Being John Malkovich, you’ll have a rough roadmap for Backrooms. Kane Parsons’ otherworldly film is a rabbit hole within a rabbit hole, that plunges into a reality that seems determined to resist explanation. It’s weird, bizarre, and relentlessly trippy. Every turn down an endless hallway deepens the mystery, layering intrigue upon intrigue. Chiwetel Ejiofor delivers a grounded performance as Clark, an ordinary man struggling to hold his life together until he stumbles upon the extradimensional space that manifests as a warped photocopy of his deteriorating world. The mythology surrounding Backrooms feels as vast and infinite as the labyrinth itself. Is it meant to be fully understood? Maybe not. But that uncertainty is what makes the film captivating. Rather than offering answers, Backrooms invites audiences to lose themselves in the unsettling wonder of the unknown. The journey is impossible to forget.
I really like this movie it was jump scares, and just the perfect type of scary movie for teenagers my age. I would definitely watch it again and I really wish it was a part two.

Filmed like a Blair Witch movie but so incredibly boring. I kept waiting for something to happen or for any single bit of information that we were shown to be explained, but… Nothing. I fell asleep for the last five minutes, but that’s okay because it didn’t make any difference at all. Smart for the creator to get such a YouTube following to come and see it with such a small budget. Other than that, it was just not good at all.
So many questions unanswered.
It was boring as hell!!
What a waste of time.
The concept of endless, haphazard spaces where you can't find your way out and get chased...that's great. But it goes so hard into the subconscious and therapy land that it doesn't make any sense.

It takes a lot of thought, but in doing so you get a great appreciation of the movie. The horror was tense and palpable, and maybe a little more clarification would've helped out general audiences. Overall enjoyed 👍

It was alright. Definitely worth the watch but I wouldn’t pay to see it because honestly it was boring. It was 2 hours but all the plot and interesting stuff could’ve fit into 1 hour, and it just wasn’t very scary. There was so much buildup time just for some mildly scary scenes. The only reason it gets 1 and a half stars is because the concept was interesting.

It's like an amalgamation of the Shining, 1408 and Insidious. Decent movie, but nothing really exciting or noteworthy in my opinion. I like Adam Scott and the hotel atmosphere is ok, but just kind of slow and didn't hit the mark with me.

Nowhere near as good as Oddity. It has style and atmosphere, but the story feels undercooked. Honestly, the film‑within‑the‑film that frames the whole thing seemed far more compelling — I found myself wishing that were the actual movie.

A good movie for afternoon TV. I have zero clue why it has such specifically high ratings. Honestly it's probably because of the cast. Nothing wrong with it, but it was literally just one of those b grade movies you would watch on a day off school. There is something wrong /corrupt about this website. The ratings are falsely high. Genre... Its suspense, not really horror.

Hokum is an atmospheric horror/mystery with a great, fail-proof setting - an old haunted hotel around Halloween. Even a marginal story or execution is likely to do ok with that Shining/1408 backdrop. Unfortunately, that’s what we get with Hokum. This is the third horror/mystery by the director of Caveat and Oddity, and I feel both of those films are superior to Hokum. Part of the problem is how unlikable the main character is. Much like in The Shining and 1408, he’s a troubled writer who drinks and must confront his past and his demons at the hotel. But the mystery of what happens to the missing girl isn’t all that mysterious, and has little or nothing to do with him. Great movies stay with you and leave you thinking about the meaning or how they end. After Hokum I could barely remember how it ended or why the hotel was haunted at all. Maybe I need a second viewing. But both Caveat and Oddity had a more focused story, with better characters.

I've felt that this film is unfortunately overrated. It had great ingredients to make a great film, but I feel it falls short of its potential. The script and the editing in particular don't have the grace that a horror film needs to immerse you into its atmosphere.

Supernatural thriller, with twists and turns that don't really go anywhere except predictable places. Jump scares and disturbing atmosphere (at times) save this from being a one star movie. The protagonist is a complete dick until the final act.
It’s not a haunting movie. It’s a psychological drama with a splash of horror. Overrated and lacklustre.

Hokum prides itself on its atmosphere, but lacks dimension, profound characters and design in the way its characters move through the world. They simply exist, moving in a videogame-like manner, as if controlled from outside and operating automatically. There is no intriguing element to make you care about them, particularly the empty and cynical protagonist. The haunted motif of one of the victims' voice recorders is a missed chance to create an appealing connection to the past. The second part of the film grabs your interest, but the mystery and horror quickly evaporate as there is no depth.
Not bad, best screamer

After dropping the ball near the end of 2024's Oddity, Hokum solidifies McCarthy as a director with great technical expertise, in great need of a decent writer. While the film looks and sounds impressive, with moody sets and suspenseful camera angles, the script is so chunky and poorly stitched together that it borders on sophomoric, and the jump-scares never manage to be the least bit scary.

Project Hail Mary was everything I hoped for and more. In a time when so many sci-fi movies feel repetitive, this one delivered a genuinely unique story that kept me invested from start to finish. What surprised me most was how much heart the movie had. It’s not just about space, science, or saving the world—it’s about friendship, hope, and doing the right thing even when the odds seem impossible. The emotional moments felt earned, and by the end I found myself genuinely moved. The story is clever, engaging, and filled with a sense of wonder that reminded me why I love science fiction in the first place. Critics and audiences have praised its mix of heart, humor, and optimism, and I completely understand why. Many reviews describe it as a crowd-pleasing adventure with real emotion, and that’s exactly how it felt to me. This is the kind of movie that leaves you feeling good when the credits roll. Smart, emotional, funny, and unforgettable. Rating: 9/10

Easily the best film in 2026. I saw this film in IMAX. It was a great experience.

I don't know why this got rave reviews. I was expecting something similar to The Martian (same author), but instead I got Airplane! in space. The jokes are terrible, they don't land, and they're not delivered well. There are too many of them- it's almost slapstick, especially in the first half of the movie. The ideas in the movie are really good- astrophage and all that. The premise is fun- one man to save the planet while sacrificing himself (not breaking new ground, but I'm here for it). Visually very nice. In Arrival, the method by which she learned to communicate with the aliens was a little contrived, but it was enough to suspend disbelief. Here, the alien learns an incredible English vocabulary (including slang) miraculously. We are shown how Grace learns the alien's language, but not how Rocky learns to understand English. I was kindof bored. The whole thing is mediocre at best and the tone is just wrong. I guess the bar is low for a blockbuster these days.

Truly terrible. A huge let down from Phil Lord and Christopher Miller. Impossible to get invested in the quip machines that are the characters in this film. It masquerades as a science fiction epic. In actuality, it is a weak buddy film set in space, with hilariously poor grounding in reality - a departure from the book, and the superbly directed The Martian. Very disappointed after hearing good things. Cannot recommend. Much better experiences of the same themes in the form of Arrival and Interstellar.
beautiful book, and they did a great job putting into a movie. i finished the book a day before watching and i STILL cried and witnessed all the tension it was amazing :))

This film made me so sad. I had low expectations after reading reviews, but it was even worse than I feared. I’m a huge fan of Brett Goldstein as an actor and writer, and I know he can play a great romantic lead, but not here. All of You was just brilliant and heartbreaking, and I watched it at least 5 times. I found it hard to reconcile that Office Romance came from the same source. What a cringeworthy script and giant waste of talent, from the leads to the supporting cast (Amy Sedaris and Betty Gilpin tried valiantly but couldn’t rescue this mess). It was lame and crude and lacked any suspense and was just wretched from start to finish.
Followed the formula of old school romance… but plot and character chemistry was non existent..effort was there but didn’t feel authentic and Jeffifer Lopez loves herself too much to be relatable
Oof. I'm pretty sure Ted Lasso would have asked all of you to take a lap. Please leave bad slapstick, horrible writing and fake chemistry to AI.

First time I watched it I thought it was ok. The next day I found myself wanting to rewatch it and really enjoyed it. I missed so many of the jokes the first time around. Jennifer Lopez is the queen of a romcom for a reason. Would recommend.

I really was enjoying this movie, then it started turning sour. I beleive they were trying for a more mature Hallmark movie but overshot the adult material. The office scene where they shook hands and he got excited was a bit funny but unnecessary. The birthing scene was shocking once and then over the top the second time. These aspects in another movie like a dramady might work but just didn't fit in a romcom. Perhaps comedic to some, but not romantic at all. I would be interested in seeing a UK perspective, perhaps I just don't get the British humor...

Absolutely disgusting. Saw a scene of this woman giving birth, nothing was covered. I can't unsee this crap now. Do not watch if you hate seeing people giving birth like you were at an hospital, because you see it twice! The name is very misleading, there is no romance in this movie. The industry is doing verrrryy bad, I saw many great actors and how awful they could not find better!

Michael was a very enjoyable movie and one of the better music biopics I’ve seen. The performances were excellent, especially Jaafar Jackson, who captured Michael’s look, energy, and stage presence in a way that never felt like an impersonation. The music sequences were easily the highlight of the film and reminded me why Michael Jackson became one of the biggest entertainers in history. Many audience reviews praised these performances and the emotional connection to the music, and I felt the same way. My only real complaint is that I wanted even more of those musical moments. Every time the movie recreated an iconic performance or focused on the creative process behind the songs, it was at its best. I found myself wishing the film spent more time there. Overall, Michael is emotional, entertaining, and a great celebration of the King of Pop’s legacy. It left me smiling, singing the songs afterward, and wanting more. Rating: 8.5/10

The Perfect Neighbor is a devastating documentary about preventable tragedy, institutional hesitation and the violence of warning signs ignored. Geeta Gandbhir builds the film from real recordings and body cam footage, removing narration so the evidence unfolds with cold clarity. Its power lies in repetition. Calls, minor conflicts and attempts to calm the situation accumulate until what once seemed manageable reveals itself as dangerous. The film shows how tragedy can grow not from invisibility, but from a failure to treat visible escalation with urgency. What remains is not only grief, but frustration at everything that was not done when intervention was still possible. The documentary offers no comfort. Justice may arrive, but it cannot repair the life already lost or erase the systems that allowed danger to become normal.
Really troubling documentary to watch. Not a feelgood time by any stretch but man as you watch the story unfold you just get more tense (and frustrated) by the second.

This story NEEDED to be shared with the world! I’ve never felt so affected and moved by a documentary. I really hope all the families who were affected by this tragedy are doing fine now. What a loss and what a mind blowing crazy story. Thank you for sharing this with the world. I definitely recommend this documentary!

This will make you pause and really evaluate so much about racism, the right to bear arms, and who should and shouldn't be able to hold a gun license. This documentary tells the story so well and you keep thinking "that didn't just happen." Yet it did and changed so many lives forever.

I watched The Perfect Neighbor and it really stuck with me. What starts as an ordinary neighborhood conflict slowly becomes tense and unsettling. The real-life footage makes everything feel incredibly personal and heartbreaking. It's not an easy watch, but it's a powerful documentary that stayed on my mind long after it ended.
A deep insight into our world, very beautifully done.

powerful documentary about racism and stand your ground laws. I truly believe that had it not been for the journalism and activism surrounding this case that she would have been cleared of the charges.

Intriguing and deeply heartbreaking. I wasn’t expecting a found footage-style film at the Oscars, and that immediately caught me off guard in a good way. The first hour leaves me cold — not bored, just emotionally detached. But everything changes once the dramatic climax arrives. Suddenly, I’m fully invested in the moral and ethical debates Gandbhir presents, constantly questioning who is right and what I would do in that situation. By the end, the film completely wins me over. It’s effective, emotionally devastating, and never longer or denser than it needs to be. Out of all the nominated documentaries, this was easily my favorite.
It was a truly genuine and emotional experience.
Engaging, if not disturbing, from start to to finish.

With a premise that centers around a group that blurs the lines between good/bad, right/wrong, legal/criminal operations, its somewhat ironic that this entire film suffers from much the same duality. Some wonderful actors given a terrible script and equally dull performances, attempts to be slick and stylish juxtaposed with lame attempts at humour and action sequences decades behind the current standard. It has a few Guy Ritcie trademarks but carries absolutely none of the wit or charm of his earlier work. Grey is a largely dull colour, which makes it an incredibly apt title for this snoozefest
Fun movie - very good actors - interesting plot - well done - excellent directing - I recommend this movie.
What happened to Guy? He made so amazaing entertaining movies. And now? Have the big directors lost the connection to the audience? Is there a "making boring movies" flu going on?
Great film to pass time. Watch it for the quirky conversations and tight action sequences.
Definitely must see movie. Very exiting and great Guy Ritchie type of action. Would love to see a part 2 see the continuation of this story and the characters.

"In the Grey" is exactly the kind of formuliac film which indian film industry (usually called 'Bollywood') used to churn out evey two months during the 1980s and 90s. If you sit in an adjecent room and just listen to it, you will understand everything that is happening in the meaningless meshup of happenings happening on the screen. The film also proves how the Red Sea Fund is burning millions on useless projects. It also proves that so called big names like Henry Cavil, Gyllenhall and Rosamund Pike work just for money, nothing else.
Loved this film ! Whoever marketed the film did a terrible job because the film is awesome but no one saw it
A great not thinking movie for a lazy evening.

Quite enjoyable movie, fun and entertaining nothing special in the range of Guy Ritchie but still excellent for a night. Will i ever see it again ? probably not but made its work. Casting was top notch, maybe Eiza not so credible as master schemer but anyhow acceptable. Timing of events totally messed up without any real development of full acts, people travelling back and forth the world for their missing like only few hours went, and legal trials happening almost REAL TIME (i wish that could be actually the case !!). This film should have developed over several months (if not years) to accomplish what they went through. (half a star removed)
I really enjoyed the movie and what really swayed me to watch it, was that all the so called "critcs" gave it a bad review. It was action packed, and a real entertaining flick.

To be clear, John Carney's "Once" and "Begin Again" are two of my favorite movies about musical connections. Both are 5 stars and both soundtracks are terrific. THIS however, is a slog from start to finish. The background track pummels the dialog throughout. And the big "song" everyone is so worked up about is a sappy little ditty with a moronic hook. No, I did not like the music but the premise and overall story weren't much better.

Power Ballad is one of the most underrated films of the year. Despite having recognizable stars and a solid marketing campaign, it never seemed to receive the wide release it deserved, which is a shame because the movie is genuinely entertaining. The biggest strength is the chemistry between Paul Rudd and Nick Jonas. Rudd takes a more restrained approach than he usually does in comedies, allowing the character to feel more natural and grounded, while Jonas brings authenticity and charisma to the musical side of the story. The supporting cast does its job, but this is clearly Rudd and Jonas’s film from beginning to end. Their dynamic keeps the story moving, and the music is memorable enough to stay with you after the credits roll. Overall, Power Ballad is a fun, heartfelt movie that balances comedy and music well. It may eventually find a larger audience on streaming, but it is absolutely worth checking out. The songs are catchy, and the theater experience adds value.

With a fairly predictable and age-old plot, the reliance is on the casting, acting, and subplots, generally. Music can help. These were finely done. There were gaps such as having no prior recording. Still, well worth watching and a good date movie.
An entertaining and delightfully simple watch, Power Ballad is anchored by the chemistry between the cast and a music-based story that manages to be funny and heartfelt.

Great acting from all of the cast. Very realistic. Heartfelt. and brings home the idea that while money is important, what is most important is having the ones we love. That this is what makes us real. Not money or fame.

Exit 8 delivers a uniquely unsettling experience built on a simple but clever concept. The entire film maintains a constant sense of unease, where even small details feel important and suspicious. Instead of relying on jump scares or heavy action, it focuses on psychological tension and atmosphere. Creepy, minimalistic, and surprisingly immersive—it stays in your mind long after it ends.
Ground hog day without the comedy.

It’s been a long time since I’ve watched a Japanese horror film and Exit 8 was such a fun return to the genre. I was somewhat familiar with the game it’s based on, but I’d never actually sat down and played it myself. I was honestly worried the movie might lean a little too goofy, but it did an excellent job of taking a very simple, almost cartoonish concept and turning it into something genuinely tense and creepy. I found myself whispering things like, "No, turn back—you missed that anomaly!" throughout the movie. It’s also the perfect length and sticks the landing with a really satisfying conclusion. I’d definitely recommend it to any horror fan, and I’ll be adding this one to my collection as soon as I can.

Not much needed to make this an excellent psychological thrill ride. Phenomenal acting with so little words/dialogue. Pissed me off how it took a little child to finally get them out of the subway, I like to think I’d have fared a little better.
i watched this right after playing the game and im shocked that how amazing this is and the depth they added to the story

The storytelling is amazing. The movie does a great job building dread through each character, and it adapts the video game really well. The backstory they showed was good, and I love the first person view throughout the whole movie!

It is very clearly a movie based on a video game and I always judge them a little diffrent then the rest - but I'm gonna be very honest with you for the first half I thought it was just going to be another one of those but then "The Eye" scene happened and it was unironically so beautiful that it was worth all the money I spend on it

i just watched the movie and it's honestly really unsettling in a good way. the whole movie traps you in this tiny submarine deep under a strange, blood red ocean and the isolation does most of the horror work. Its slow , quiet, and really tense rather than full of jump scares.

This movie reminds me of another favourite suspense/horror film called "Savageland" in one key principle…. Less is definitely More!! This saying most definitely applies towards this work of art… It proves that an interesting concept, imagination and hard work does not need a massive budget, cast or landscape to merit success! If a word does come to mind to describe this film, then I suppose Claustrophobic works (to maximum effect)..

The visuals hurt this film more than the lack of building a clear story progression, leaving the whole movie feeling like it never brings the audience along in on what the climax is trying to present.
Well made. It is impressive what one dedicated man can do with a low budget.

love this movie, this movie is the definition of stuck in the most uncomfortable situation while you're trying your best to survive even if it seems impossible, it's successfully deliver it. The ambience, the BLOOD, and the mystery outside the submarine. The story is top notch, for me at least. I like the mystery, and it's the one that kept me watching despite the slow-burn, the twists are insane. i also loves the "markiplier" personality, like simon kept saying cuss words, it's like seeing markiplier do a let's play, but it is real LMAO

Hoppers was a good film. The plot was refreshingly different from that of a typical children’s movie. The film explores themes such as activism, family, emotional regulation, and personal growth. It also delivers plenty of humor, making it entertaining for both children and adults. One of the movie’s strengths is its unexpected twist, which helps keep the story engaging from beginning to end. Overall, I would recommend Hoppers as a great family movie night pick. It offers meaningful themes, strong comedy, and an engaging story that audiences of all ages can enjoy.

The perfect simulation of pristine nature, while real nature is being destroyed in every possible way. Is this where we've ended up? If you want to experience the miracles of nature, do you have to watch CGI? Because there's no nature left in real life?

I'm used to Sacha Baron Cohen's unique brand of comedy and his ability to deliver films packed with satire, social commentary, and uncomfortable truths. This time, he tackles one of the most debated topics of modern society: gender roles and expectations. Ladies First explores a simple but effective premise: what if the world were reversed, with men and women swapping social positions? The result is an interesting exercise in empathy, and that's where Cohen extracts the film's greatest strengths. More than a fictional scenario, it often feels like a reflection of real-world dynamics viewed through a different lens. A smart and entertaining satire that encourages empathy and self-reflection, even if it doesn't leave the lasting impact of Cohen's strongest films.

Commedia che analizza il sessismo nel mondo del lavoro e che alterna momenti di vera frustrazione che può sembrare anche molto vicina allo spettatore di ambo i sessi, a momenti in cui in verità la parte demenziale prevale troppo. Purtroppo, dopo un inizio tutto sommato promettente, il film si disinnesca da solo portando dialoghi e gag poco incisive e dall'umorismo rivedibile. Anche il finale è abbastanza moscio e prevedibile, di fatto limitando il film al ruolo di commedia politically correct; senza andare più a fondo in nulla.
So funny my man laughed his ass off too. Very clever. For all our daughters!

So.....spoilers. The ending didn't sit well with me. It felt that he essentially went after Alex because of his romantic interest. Nothing else changed for any of the other female characters minus his secretary. It felt he went to get Alex back, to "win" her. And in order to get back to his world, he had to beat out a woman for her job. That she already had. And the most unrealistic part? Her getting a call about her kid's tooth. If the genders are supposed to be swapped, then it would have been the father dealing with it. Idk. It was boring and strange sometimes. Wouldn't recommend.

Since the primary purpose of a horror movie is always to be scary, and the tension present in Passenger is nerve-shredding at times, I would deem this movie a bigger success than many reviews have made it out to be. I'd never have guessed that either lead wasn't American, so fair play to them, but Jacob Scipio never quite acts as petrified as he ought to considering the relentless terror he's faced with. The titular 'Passenger,' whilst undermining the movie with arbitrary actions - seriously, he had countless opportunities to take care of that young couple before the fiery finale - is a figure likely to appear in the nightmares of viewers, particularly those who know the uneasiness of being the only earthly inhabitants for miles around. The trailers should have been retracted when it became clear that they ruined literally the entire opening scene and much beyond that - everybody who watched them knew exactly when the movie's best jump scare was coming! Familiar, sure, but good effort.
It’s a okay movie not as good as the trailer made it out to be.

This was a wonderful, wonderful film one of the best films I’ve seen in a very long time. Sally Field was wonderful.

Remarkably Bright Creatures didn’t fully convince me with the octopus as an observer and narrator, but Sally Field and Lewis Pullman were outstanding. Their performances — and the stories they carried about relationships, resilience, and the quiet struggles people live through — were what stayed with me. In a world saturated with violence, division, and constant bad news, the film felt like a reminder of something simpler and more essential. It was genuinely uplifting. More than ever, I think we need films that focus on connection — on people looking out for one another, rebuilding trust, and finding their way back to community in a post‑pandemic world. I'd recommend it.

ASTONISHING MOVIE IT GIVE US DIFFERENT PERSPECTIVE FOR THE WORLD

I Swear is the kind of movie that stays with you long after you've left the theater. Based on a true story, the film follows the struggles of a young man being diagnosed with Tourette's Syndrome in his childhood. In a time when mental illness and disabilities were not discussed but ostracized, John Davidson managed to find his path in life to help others suffering like him. The powerful messages, education, laughs, tears... all of it gives the viewer a better understanding of the uncurable disease. In the end, it is a must watch.
Absolutely great acting by Aramayo Robert. Really honestly moving.

I swear it's one of those movies that you absolutely want your kids to see. Not because it's entertaining and it keeps them quiet for an hour or so but because it teaches them A Life Lesson about how people are treated and things that people go through every day. that should make them cognizant of the way they treat everyone. Now let me get off my soapbox. An amazing tearjerker comedy drama lighthearted fun happy sad movie about the trials and tribulations of Tourette's.
Hilarious, stressful & emotional

Absolutely amazing! This movie tells a powerful story about a man living with Tourette syndrome and the many struggles and challenges that come with the condition. It is deeply moving, even if you’ve never met someone with Tourette syndrome before. The film is incredibly inspiring and reminds us that having caring, supportive people around you can make all the difference. It delivers a beautiful message about kindness, compassion, and the impact they can have on our lives. This is a must-see movie. It will make you laugh, bring you joy, fill you with love, and probably leave you in tears. The cinematography is stunning, and the cast delivers outstanding performances. A true masterpiece from start to finish.
Watch it, you won’t regret it!

I watched the opening climbing scenes and couldn’t believe the basic inaccuracies. Ie, clipping into the wrong part of a quick draw. Not setting a friend anchor correctly into a crack. Wearing sports climbing shoes on an ice route. Wearing dachstein woolen mitts instead of modern gloves. Abseiling down together down the mountain. All ridiculous- I turned it off after the climbing- if this is the directors attention to detail then it’s not worth watching.

Exactly what’s wrong with this corporation king world we all now suffer! It happened to a friend of mine! Be went to buy a huge Dental clinic, place made £550,000 profit per year after paying everything including owners husband and wife £120,000 each! So,,, the bank handed his business plan under the table to a load of corporations and tried to push him out the deal with crap excuses! The owner wanted him to have it so told him he had five offers suddenly from big dental corporations so my friend found out!! He had to sue the bank for doing this using the owner as a witness as this isn’t legal! As a world we must make a stand before a select few buy up everything worth having to then use the rest of us as drones to service the directors and boards needs!!! They do nothing and get richer with baby soft fingers and bully tactics!! Great movie!! Illustrating the point clearly down to the law backing up the real crooks! He won but had to do ten years for the privilege of proving his point!

Now 'Dog Day Afternoon' from 1975 starring Al Pacino and John Cazale is a classic, based on the true story of a bank robbery that goes wrong, and this flick is a kind of remake? The story is different though, Bill Skarsgard stars as a desperate man who wants justice from the bank he believed ripped him off in a property deal, he takes the son of the Bank's CEO (played brilliantly by Al Pacino) wires a shotgun around his neck, demanding compensation, but most of all, an apology, something Pacino's character refuses! It's a quality film throughout, the 70's is brilliantly recreated, everything's beige, smoke fills every room, Skarsgard is brilliant, as is Dacre Montgomery as the unfortunate kidnap victim, and of course Pacino (though he sadly is only in a few scenes) This is out on Sky Movies, I'd never heard of it until seeing it advertised, and it's well worth checking out. 4/5

This is a well made film based upon a true story about a Greek land owner who decides to take matters in his own hands to get justice from the mortgage company he believes did him wrong. The acting is top notch. Bill Skarsgard is a great actor. Al Pacino is always good. The look and feel are pure 70's. Just never gets to the next level but highly watchable.
After feeling taken advantage of by a mortgage company, Tony straps a shotgun to the owner’s son. They did a good job setting the time period with aged camerawork and vintage stills.

It felt real, almost as if we were watching the kidnapping unfold in front of us. The film did a very good job recreating the look and sound of the mid-1970s, from the clothes and interiors to the media circus around the event. True crime usually has little appeal for us, but this one was unusual enough and well made enough to be worth watching.

This crime movie was great. Bill Skarsgard, Dacre Montgomery, Cary Elwes, Colman Domingo, Al Pacino, Myha'la, and the rest of the cast did a great job in this movie. This true story about Tony Kiritsis, an angry real estate developer took Richard Hall hostage because his dad who was head of Meridian Mortgage, believed he was cheated and ruined by them was dramatic and intense. If you haven't seen this movie yet, check it out sometime. It's worth watching.
Boring story. Otherwise exceptional. Great performances. Great directing. Just boring.

It felt real, almost as if we were watching the kidnapping unfold in front of us. The film did a very good job recreating the look and sound of the mid-1970s, from the clothes and interiors to the media circus around the event. True crime usually has little appeal for us, but this one was unusual enough and well made enough to be worth watching.

This crime movie was great. Bill Skarsgard, Dacre Montgomery, Cary Elwes, Colman Domingo, Al Pacino, Myha'la, and the rest of the cast did a great job in this movie. This true story about Tony Kiritsis, an angry real estate developer took Richard Hall hostage because his dad who was head of Meridian Mortgage, believed he was cheated and ruined by them was dramatic and intense. If you haven't seen this movie yet, check it out sometime. It's worth watching.
Boring story. Otherwise exceptional. Great performances. Great directing. Just boring.
Boring and pointless.
A solid drama with plenty of good performances. The story didn't particularly grip me, but the cast did.
It was all right. Good cast but predictable and kind of slow and boring. I had to fast fwd to see the end which I had an idea what it was.

Exactly what’s wrong with this corporation king world we all now suffer! It happened to a friend of mine! Be went to buy a huge Dental clinic, place made £550,000 profit per year after paying everything including owners husband and wife £120,000 each! So,,, the bank handed his business plan under the table to a load of corporations and tried to push him out the deal with crap excuses! The owner wanted him to have it so told him he had five offers suddenly from big dental corporations so my friend found out!! He had to sue the bank for doing this using the owner as a witness as this isn’t legal! As a world we must make a stand before a select few buy up everything worth having to then use the rest of us as drones to service the directors and boards needs!!! They do nothing and get richer with baby soft fingers and bully tactics!! Great movie!! Illustrating the point clearly down to the law backing up the real crooks! He won but had to do ten years for the privilege of proving his point!

Now 'Dog Day Afternoon' from 1975 starring Al Pacino and John Cazale is a classic, based on the true story of a bank robbery that goes wrong, and this flick is a kind of remake? The story is different though, Bill Skarsgard stars as a desperate man who wants justice from the bank he believed ripped him off in a property deal, he takes the son of the Bank's CEO (played brilliantly by Al Pacino) wires a shotgun around his neck, demanding compensation, but most of all, an apology, something Pacino's character refuses! It's a quality film throughout, the 70's is brilliantly recreated, everything's beige, smoke fills every room, Skarsgard is brilliant, as is Dacre Montgomery as the unfortunate kidnap victim, and of course Pacino (though he sadly is only in a few scenes) This is out on Sky Movies, I'd never heard of it until seeing it advertised, and it's well worth checking out. 4/5

This is a well made film based upon a true story about a Greek land owner who decides to take matters in his own hands to get justice from the mortgage company he believes did him wrong. I liked the original story line. The make up was awesome. I really felt like the characters were real and relatable through their shared trauma bonding. I disliked the ending. The characters make this work. The story isn’t anything groundbreaking but it’s a fun journey. Worth a watch
